shorts.content: Self-care is not selfish—it is mandatory for every mother. When mom is well, everyone benefits. You can't pour from an empty cup, and your family needs you at your best.
shorts.title: Why Self-Care Is Mandatory for Moms

shorts.content: Life is written in pencil—you have permission to pivot. Your career can evolve with motherhood. You don't have to choose between being a great mom and having professional success.
shorts.title: You Have Permission to Pivot Your Career

shorts.content: Being fully present for one hour daily gives your children more attention than most kids receive. Quality over quantity matters more than you think.
shorts.title: One Hour of Presence Changes Everything

shorts.content: Your nervous system must be regulated to lead from intuition rather than fear-based decisions. When you're calm and centered, you make better choices for your family and business.
shorts.title: Why Nervous System Health Matters for Leaders

titleOptions.curiosityGap: What Happens When Moms Stop Calling Self-Care Selfish?
titleOptions.boldStatement: Self-Care Isn't Selfish—It's Mandatory for Every Mother
titleOptions.numberBased: 5 Signs You Need to Stop the Mom Guilt Around Self-Care

description:
Think self-care is selfish? Dr. Laura Brayton is here to change your mind forever.
Discover why prioritizing yourself as a mother isn't just okay—it's mandatory for your family's wellbeing.

🎯 KEY TAKEAWAYS:
• Self-care is mandatory, not selfish—everyone benefits when mom is well
• Chiropractic care is safe throughout all three trimesters
• Preconception detox should begin 6 months before conception
• Regulate your nervous system to lead from intuition, not fear
• You have permission to pivot your career for motherhood
• One hour of presence daily gives kids more attention than most receive
• Corporate skills translate beautifully into household leadership

⏰ TIMESTAMPS:
00:00 Introduction
02:15 Why self-care isn't selfish
08:30 Chiropractic care during pregnancy
15:45 Preconception health and detox
22:10 Nervous system regulation for mothers
28:20 Permission to pivot your career
35:40 Quality vs quantity time with children
42:15 Translating corporate skills to motherhood

communityPosts.content:
What's your biggest struggle with self-care as a mother? 🤱

A) Finding the time
B) Feeling guilty about it
C) Not knowing where to start
D) Thinking it's selfish

Just dropped an episode with Dr. Laura Brayton that might change how you think about this entirely. She says self-care isn't selfish—it's MANDATORY for every mother because everyone benefits when mom is well. 💪

What's holding YOU back from prioritizing yourself?

"Life is written in pencil—you have permission to pivot." ✏️

Dr. Laura Brayton dropped this wisdom bomb in our latest episode about holistic motherhood, and I can't stop thinking about it.

How many of us feel trapped by the choices we made before we became mothers? Like we have to keep the same career, same schedule, same everything even when it no longer serves our family?

Your life can evolve. Your career can shift. You have permission to make changes that align with who you are NOW as a mother.

What would you change if you truly believed life was written in pencil? 🤔

pinnedComment: 👋 What resonated most with you from Dr. Laura's insights on holistic motherhood? Drop a comment below and let me know! Plus, don't forget to grab the full show notes and take our quiz to discover your leadership style: https://themanageher.com/blog/holistic-motherhood-pregnancy-self-care-nervous-system-health-with-dr-laura-brayton
